stories.title: Don't just sit there at halftime: a 3-minute priming protocol changes the second half
stories.teaser: 22 professional women's soccer players. Randomized controlled trial. A brief protocol combining FIFA 11+ exercises and a small-sided game improved sprint performance and attenuated jump decline compared to passive rest.
stories.source: Abreu et al. — Int J Sports Physiol Perform, 2026

stories.title: ACG Team Camp: Palm Springs
stories.teaser: 25+ professional trail runners from around the world. Five days in the Palm Springs desert. Four research studies, fireside seminars, and product sessions — where science met the mountain.

stories.title: Every Answer: Elite Miler Case Study
stories.teaser: A world-class miler embeds with the NSRL for an intensive residency of discovery. Fourteen days of concierge-level performance science — physiology, biomechanics, nutrition, and beyond.

stories.title: Inside HYROX Physiology
stories.teaser: The 3rd-place finisher at the HYROX World Championships visits the NSRL. Blood lactate, muscle oxygen, and footwear feedback — a field-to-lab look at hybrid racing demands.

stories.title: Your brain after 50km: ultramarathon racing alters executive function
stories.teaser: 76 runners. Mobile EEG. The first large-sample evidence that ultramarathon racing reduces neural activity tied to inhibitory control and attention.
stories.source: Boere et al. — Journal of Applied Physiology, 2026

stories.title: Breaking4: What Would It Take?
stories.teaser: A multi-year scientific expedition across four institutions to answer one question: what would it take for a woman to break 4 minutes in the mile? Five studies. One thread of oxygen.

stories.title: Hot baths, more oxygen: passive heat acclimation boosts VO₂max by 4.4%
stories.teaser: 5 weeks of hot water immersion increased hemoglobin mass and expanded the heart's stroke volume in trained runners — without changing their training.
stories.source: Jenkins et al. — The Journal of Physiology, 2025

stories.title: 7.65 seconds away: the physiology of a women's sub-4 minute mile
stories.teaser: A viewpoint on what it takes to break the barrier — and the 10 commentary letters it sparked from researchers worldwide.
stories.source: Osborne, Kirby et al. — Journal of Applied Physiology, 2025

stories.title: The Physiology of 4 Minutes
stories.teaser: The first study examining the complete O₂ transport cascade during running in humans — and the first to ask whether women face unique physiological challenges in closing the gap to four minutes.

stories.title: The Exeter Model: The formula for 4 minutes
stories.teaser: 21 middle-distance athletes. Lab and track testing. Two predictive models. The first mathematical framework for the 4-minute mile — and the discovery that aerobic capacity has 5.7x more impact than anaerobic reserve.

stories.title: It's Not the Hormones
stories.teaser: Oregon tested whether menstrual cycle phase changes middle-distance running performance. What actually mattered was the capacity to consume oxygen — muscle oxidative capacity and hemoglobin mass.

stories.title: The World Record Holder
stories.teaser: The aerobic engine is extraordinary — the gap is anaerobic. A full physiological assessment of the world's most prolific middle-distance runner reveals where the 4-minute barrier lives.

stories.title: Breathe. Hold. Prime: The Breath-Hold Priming Protocol
stories.teaser: Five breath holds before the gun. More hemoglobin in the blood. Higher lactate thresholds. Longer time to exhaustion. A race-day intervention that shifts the oxygen equation.
stories.source: Nike Sport Research Lab — Breaking4 Chapter 5
